headline: 4 protocol contracts catalogued on Solana; primary lending program and staging/vault programs discovered; 18 audits referenced; governance token identified

findings.text: Solana block explorers (Solscan, SolanaCompass) do not support name-tag search via HTTP like Etherscan; however, Kamino-Finance GitHub organization and primary contract addresses are directly discoverable via search.

Deployed contracts page referenced in docs at https://docs.kamino.finance/ shows market address 7u3HeHxYDLhnCoErrtycNokbQYbWGzLs6JSDqGAv5PfF; main program ID KLend2g3cP87fffoy8q1mQqGKjrxjC8boSyAYavgmjD found in official GitHub README.

Kamino website indicates 18 audits completed; specific audit PDFs and dates not accessible via fetch (security page permission denied); audit repository reference found at github.com/Kamino-Finance/audits (not accessible).
GitHub repository https://github.com/Kamino-Finance/klend contains mainnet and staging program IDs in README; kvault program for yield lending identified at https://github.com/Kamino-Finance/kvault.
Single chain: Solana mainnet only; no multichain deployments identified.

No factory enumeration performed; KaminoMarket contract on Solana may have child reserves/markets, but read API access and Solana RPC methods require chainId=501 which is not in the pinned defipunkd support list; cannot enumerate programmatically this run.

Discovered addresses classified as: KLend2g3cP87fffoy8q1mQqGKjrxjC8boSyAYavgmjD (lending program/admin), SLendK7ySfcEzyaFqy93gDnD3RtrpXJcnRwb6zFHJSh (staging program), 7u3HeHxYDLhnCoErrtycNokbQYbWGzLs6JSDqGAv5PfF (market/core contract), kvault program role unknown from docs.
All addresses trace to fetched GitHub README or developer documentation; no snippet-only citations.

rationale.verdict: Discovery phase: catalogued 4 program addresses from GitHub and developer docs on Solana mainnet; 18 audits referenced but not individually fetched; governance token KMNO identified; docs reference market contract and vault infrastructure; Solana lacks block-explorer name-tag API so enumeration incomplete. Off-chain evidence only due to defipunkd API not supporting Solana (chainId 501). Recommend next run prioritize fetching audit PDFs to extract audit dates and firm names, and programmatic enumeration of market child accounts via Solana RPC once supported.

evidence.shows: GitHub repository README lists mainnet program ID KLend2g3cP87fffoy8q1mQqGKjrxjC8boSyAYavgmjD and staging ID SLendK7ySfcEzyaFqy93gDnD3RtrpXJcnRwb6zFHJSh
GitHub repository describes kvault as open source smart contract for yield lending on Kamino Lending program
Developer documentation contains market address 7u3HeHxYDLhnCoErrtycNokbQYbWGzLs6JSDqGAv5PfF referenced in KaminoMarket.load() examples and indicates v2 modular lending infrastructure
Website header references 18 audits completed; Kamino described as unified Solana DeFi protocol for Lending, Liquidity, and Leverage
Third-party project profile confirms Kamino Lend V2 as modular lending infrastructure, KMNO as governance token, and multi-sig treasury with emergency mechanisms

unknowns: D2-offchain: Deployed addresses documentation page not directly fetchable; next run should attempt /docs/deployments, /docs/addresses or similar canonical path
D3: Audit report PDFs not accessible (kamino.com/security#security-audits requires JavaScript rendering); audit firm names, dates, and scope not enumerable this run

D6-offchain: Solana market child contracts (reserves, obligation accounts) cannot be enumerated via defipunkd API (chainId 501 not in supported list); require direct Solana RPC getProgramAccounts or Solana Compass enumeration next run
D8: kvault program address not discovered; referenced in GitHub but no on-chain program ID surfaced

protocol_metadata.about: Kamino Finance is a unified DeFi protocol on Solana integrating lending, borrowing, liquidity provision, and leverage. Users can deposit assets in Kamino Lend to earn yield via isolated lending markets, borrow against crypto collateral, or provide automated liquidity to concentrated liquidity positions. Kamino Lend V2 introduces modular market infrastructure allowing permissionless deployment of custom lending markets. The protocol is governed by KMNO token and has completed 18 independent security audits.
